task: I need to send a quick note to a couple of coworkers about a process change we’re rolling out tomorrow. Can you help me draft something clear and easy to read for a busy workday?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, clear note you can send:

**Subject:** Process Change Starting Tomorrow

Hi team,

Just a quick heads-up: we’re rolling out a process change starting tomorrow. Please follow the new steps as outlined and use the updated instructions going forward.

If anything is unclear, check with me before proceeding. Thanks for staying on top of it.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- more casual
- more formal
- shorter, like a text/Teams message

user_simulator.text: That’s fine, but make it a little more specific. I don’t want it sounding vague like “new steps as outlined” — people will skim right past that. Give me a version that says the change starts tomorrow, keep it short, and make it clear they need to use the updated process instead of the old one. A plain Teams-style note would be better than a formal email.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plain Teams-style version:

> Quick heads-up: the process change starts tomorrow. Please use the updated process from that point on and don’t follow the old steps. If anything is unclear, check with me before you proceed.

If you want, I can make it even more direct or add the specific process name.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and clear enough. The “don’t follow the old steps” part is the bit I wanted in there.
